title: chrysophyllum roxburghii
intro: Chrysophyllum roxburghii, also known as the Rose Apple tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chrysophyllum roxburghii include treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
